Close-to-screen posture was noted in most event windows where face visibility was available, including early, middle, and late relative positions.
uncertainty_or_counter_evidence: Screen gaze ratio and maximum continuous gaze duration were not available.
Screen orientation proxy was motion-confounded and not reliably estimated.
Hands were not observed in sampled windows, so hand-device interaction or touch input was not confirmed.
Motion confounding was high and observed in most sampled windows, limiting interpretation of posture stability and device-region activity.
Stable screen context was observed only in a small minority of sampled windows.
Eye-closure proxy was not observed, blink-rate trend was mostly stable, and sampled eye openness was generally typical in most event windows.
The evidence is privacy-filtered frame-proxy text only and is not sufficient for diagnosis, emotion inference, or addiction conclusion.
